mentions.note: Cramer's preferred gold miner—known for consistency; owns it personally; likes it better than Newmont; gold mining critical portfolio position.
Owns in travel trust; terrific situation with defense budget tailwinds; Cramer notes regret not owning gold stock or oil, but Boeing holding strong.
Interesting fintech (rare positive); Cramer wants to interview CEO Ben Stoto to explain why better than others—hold for deeper analysis.
More forward-looking than Exxon; Cramer's preferred E&P play for new buyers; Cramer regrets selling his own oil; recommends owning oil exposure (Chevron for new positions).
Down 8% to 52-week low despite 3% yield; Cramer waiting for lower mortgage rates before adding; regards as one of two most problematic positions in portfolio (with AT&T) due to ICE concerns.

Pure senior housing IPO (100% exposure, vs. Ventas 60%, Welltower 85%); company-operated not just landlord; 10,422 units, 34 communities; 69% FL/TX, 9% Philadelphia; 15 life-plan communities = longer resident tenure; 2.5% yield; Healthpeak retains control/management; smaller than Ventas/Welltower = faster growth potential; right in valuation middle (37x last year FFO); newer story = riskier but 'leap of faith I'd be comfortable making.'
CEO Jim Taiclet does great job; aerospace/defense down 3 weeks = terrific opportunity; world changed since Feb 28—defense stocks now attractive under Trump's bigger budget push.

Largest gold miner with female CEO (first in 104 years); Cramer has CEO on show this weekend; caller interested in Newmont; Cramer open to story but prefers Agnico; gold mining exposure important long-term.
Not a science project but no near-term profit prospects important for stock—avoid.

Pipeline operator positioned for LNG growth to 30 BCF by 2030 (+12 BCF/day); Bakken has 5,000 wells to drill; expected crude exports 14–15M barrels/day (vs. prior 3.5M); strong capital allocation (dividends, debt paydown, buybacks); 4.85% yield.
Boring company with no yield, no growth, no up stock momentum—pass.

mentions.company: Polarcoin Beverage / Polar Bear (PBR stock ticker context unclear)
mentions.note: Parabolic move up; Cramer won't recommend parabolic charts—too straight up; wait for pullback entry.
At $15 (down from $30, $50 highs); didn't like at higher price; 'cut and run'—lukewarm hold; wait for clarity.

New CEO Nick Fink (ex-Fortune Brands Jim Beam); reports Wed after-close; top beer Modelo; liquor challenged by GLP-1s but beer seeing subtle turn and ready-to-drink cocktails turning; Cramer thinks worthwhile to buy ahead; 'if true, STZ is the one you want to be in.'
$150M insider buying; 22% growth; faces Amazon competition; Cramer willing to 'bless' at $22 entry; needs couple quarters of proof.

Senior housing REIT led by Deb Cafaro; ~20% average annual gain 2+ decades; 2,100% gain since 2000 (9,200% with dividends); 112% 3-year return; 60% of earnings senior housing, 80K units; 2.5% yield; cheapest valuation (26x 2026 FFO) with best yield; 'the one who brought us'—best long-term hold in senior housing space.

Senior housing REIT; 202% 3-year total return; 85% senior housing, 100K+ units; aggressive acquisitions working well; 46x 2026 FFO—most expensive in group; 37x last year FFO; lowest dividend yield (~1.5%); good story but pricier than Ventas.
Caller holds at ~$160; Cramer recommends staying in position; up 34% YTD; prefers Chevron but doesn't recommend switching; E&P plays attractive post-oil-weakness.
